column6: I recently suggested this Global Correction, but this instance proves that the Global Correction is yet to be made!  Here is the correction: "ā a." —> "ā a".  The Specific Transformation is in regard to the Erroneous Abbreviation  "a."  in italics, which indicates "adjective".  Please note there will NEVER be an adjective indicated after a Long Vowel "ā", which is strictly a Feminine Noun, according to AP Protocol.

Please Add the Compound Indicated Here in the Window Display: "kāṣṭhatakṣakaḥ". This compound deserves to be a Searchable Headword, located in the Hierarchy List Display, in order to Fully Honor and Pay Resect to AP57.

column5: MW says "(ā́), f.", so should have accent. Same for "(ī́), f." at ID 177288.  [doubt: MW does list them as H2 words without accent at 177649 and 177694].
